name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: We need you on this one, Peter
body:
Seriously... | Peter --

We all know that Donald Trump wakes up every morning, unlocks his Samsung Galaxy*, and scrolls through Twitter. Probably while watching Fox & Friends.

So when there’s a headline with his name in it, we know that there’s a pretty good chance that he’s probably going to see it, personally.**

That means, in about a week, he's going to see a news story that describes how the two campaigns did in terms of fundraising for the month of August.

And I’d love to think of that moment as an opportunity -- our personal invitation to dictate how Trump begins his day.

Want to be part of it? All you have to do is **[chip in to get a free sticker](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=13&ms=MjYzMzE3MjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzUxODg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)** right now!

That all said, Peter, this really could go two ways.

We’ve raised more money than Trump every month of this campaign so far. But in July, he came closer to matching us than he’s ever been before.

And for the past few days, our fundraising has fallen off just a bit. We just haven’t been hitting our projections.***

So...instead of sending Trump a message about how strong this team is, we could fall short. If we don’t meet our goal, Donald Trump could wake up buoyed by a headline that says his team beat this one -- right as the election kicks into high gear.

And good lord, would I like to avoid that.

So can we make a decision, all of us together, to start Donald Trump’s morning with the kind of headline that leaves him grinding his teeth?

**Donate, Peter. We’re all counting on you!** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjYzMzE3MjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzUxODg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you (seriously),

Christina

Christina Reynolds
Deputy Communications Director
Hillary for America

*Yep! Donald Trump uses an Android. We looked it up.

**I mean seriously, does anyone doubt for a minute that Donald J. Trump is the kind of guy who reads his own press clippings?

***Though lately, we’ve been coming a lot closer because this team’s been stepping up, and I can’t tell you how much we appreciate that.

|
**Hillary needs you now more than ever -- chip in $1 and be one of the more than 2 million grassroots donors standing with her:**

| [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjYzMzE3MjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzUxODg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Marlon Marshall, HillaryClinton.com
subject: Peter, we're almost there -- will you sign up to volunteer before midnight?
body:
| Peter --

>From talking to our supporters, we’ve found that the #1 reason people don’t volunteer is simple: they weren’t asked.

**[So today, as part of HFA National Volunteer Day, we’re asking: Will you add your name and be one of the 50,000 volunteer sign ups we’re looking for before midnight tonight?](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=17&ms=MjYzMzE5MTcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzU1MTI2S0&mt=1&rt=0) **

But there’s another reason folks shared with us: People aren’t sure exactly what volunteering involves, or have a mistaken idea of what volunteering is like. So I want to make sure you have an absolutely crystal clear picture of what to expect.

The main goal for every volunteer is to have a one-on-one conversation with a voter about their plans for Election Day. This is your best chance to change someone’s mind if they’re on the fence, or even convince them to vote if they were thinking of staying home -- and it’s proven to increase voter turnout by as much as 20 percent. There are a lot of different ways to go about it:
> * Going door-to-door -- studies show a door knock is the most effective tactic to get a voter to the polls
> * Calling a voter on the phone (**[which you can do from anywhere](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=14&ms=MjYzMzE5MTcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzU1MTI2S0&mt=1&rt=0)**)
> * Connecting with voters online through social media and chat
> * Spending an afternoon covering a table in a public space or fair with information about the candidate
> * Helping register voters
>

During that conversation, no matter the venue, your job is simple: Talk to them about your values, why YOU care about Hillary, and what her presidency would mean to you. That’s all you need -- sincerity and enthusiasm.

Here are things we will NEVER ask you to do during a conversation with a voter:
> * Ask for money -- we never, ever, ever solicit donations in this way
> * Explain the in-depth details of Hillary’s platform -- we have tons of great content on our website to direct people to
> * Engage with someone who’s rude or threatening -- you’re free to hang up or walk away from any interaction
> * Ask you to do something you’re not physically or mentally capable of
>

If those activities aren’t your cup of tea, there are so many other ways to help, even from home:
> * Host other volunteers who’ve traveled to your city to get out the vote
> * Host debate watch parties
> * Host grassroots fundraisers
> * Host a calling party
>

I know it might be easy to just chip in a few dollars and call it a day -- and **[you should do that, too](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=18&ms=MjYzMzE5MTcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzU1MTI2S0&mt=1&rt=0)**! But the truth is, every dollar you give us is a dollar we might have to use to hire someone to do the tasks I listed above. If we have more volunteers on board to get out the vote, we can devote our resources to campaign activities like ad buys or expanding our operation into states we might have a shot at winning.

**Your support has gotten us this far. Now take it to the next level. What do you think, Peter? Will you make a commitment to volunteer before midnight tonight? We are very close to our goal of 50,000 volunteer sign ups. Help us beat it:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjYzMzE5MTcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzU1MTI2S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks, Peter -- now let’s go and win this election.

Marlon

Marlon Marshall
Director of States and Political Engagement
Hillary for America

|
**We need to connect with every voter if we’re going to put Hillary in the White House. If you can't give your time, chip in $1 to help us invest in our ground game:**

| [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjYzMzE5MTcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0MzU1MTI2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Heritage Action for America
subject: Will you answer the call?
body:
Hi there,
After eight years of the Obama Presidency, I know you are tired of fighting.
Tired of fighting faceless bureaucrats that interfere in every aspect of your life.Tired of fighting elected officials who say one thing, but do another once they get to Washington. It seems like the odds are always stacked against you.
Regardless of who is president next year it is up to us -- together -- to ensure that a strong, conservative, legislative branch stands up to special interests and presidential power grabs. The best way to stop a renegade president is to fight back. To fight back with facts. To fight with passion and vigor at every turn as a unified conservative voice.
**[That is why we need you to become a Heritage Action Sentinel.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37194987:WK-8zpbN5:m:1:1320601823:EBC3EE8E335D5E3878461EE17792736A:r)** Only with a large volunteer army of citizen activists can we hope to protect our Republic from those that would undo all that is great about our country.
As a Heritage Action activist you will join a group of 14,000 committed conservatives that volunteer their time to holding members of Congress accountable. By attending town halls, writing letters to the editor, or simply contacting your member of Congress you can be helping the conservative movement.
**[>>>Answer the call to join the 14,000 patriots fighting for freedom every day. ](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37194987:WK-8zpbN5:m:1:1320601823:EBC3EE8E335D5E3878461EE17792736A:r)**
Now is the time to harden your resolve and fight back -- and the best way to do that is through the Heritage Action Sentinel Program.
Thank you,
Jessica
_Jessica Anderson_
_Grassroots Director_
_Heritage Action for America_

name: Ben Carson
subject: America: Giving up on God?
body:
Peter,

A very troubling new survey just released by the Pew Research
Center finds, in a nutshell, that Americans are giving up on both
God and miracles.

The study identifies a massive 49-percent of those surveyed as
"nones," or those who don't identify with a religious group, say
they left their church because they simply "don't believe." Some
said it was because they "don't believe in miracles."

As a Christian, and as an American who knows the paramount role
that faith played in the founding of our nation, this is
alarming. But more importantly, this motivates me.

This motivates me to show how faith can lead to a better life for
us as individuals, and how embracing our Judeo-Christian values
can lead to a better future for our entire nation...and it
doesn't take a miracle.

What it takes is each of us...you and me...to act. Christians
have a real opportunity to actually act on our beliefs and our
convictions. We must shun cynicism and indifference and exercise
our voices. This is not only our right, but it is our
responsibility as a person of faith.

That's what My Faith Votes is all about, and 2016 is our
opportunity to show that it's not all for naught. I ask that you
continue to generously support our work because if we don't act
on our convictions, we'll see more and more people giving up on
faith. They'll believe that it just doesn't matter, because the
faithful aren't willing to follow through on their values.

myfaithvotes.z2systems.com/np/clients/myfaithvotes/donation.jsp?campaign=68

The good news is that we've been seeing an enormous response to
our various calls to action.

For example, this week we held another interactive My Faith Votes
LIVE TeleForum -- a key feature of our 2016 strategy -- right
from my living room with actor Kirk Cameron. In all, an
astounding 317,000 people participated. We were thrilled, and we
couldn't have done it without your generous, ongoing support. I
hope you will continue to join and support these events.

myfaithvotes.z2systems.com/np/clients/myfaithvotes/donation.jsp?campaign=68

Peter, I will not be surprised if you hear more about this new
Pew survey on the news because the numbers are fairly striking.

And surely, those who hope to whitewash God from our history and
transform America into a more secular, European-style nation will
use this to demoralize us.

But like me, I hope you see this as an opportunity and as
motivation to get more involved.

Thank you for your time today, and God bless you.

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

name: Patricia Scott for Katie McGinty
subject: things changed
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-4273185837509771264/-7280326083365306368?refcode=e20160825&refcode2=e20160825nd&amount=25)
---
Hi there --

I grew up in West Philly, and I remember the lesson my mother taught me as a little girl: _" If you work hard in life, you can make it through anything."_ I still live by those words.

But a lot has changed, too:

My mother got sick. My young grandson moved in with me. I used to have a job that paid well -- but it meant long hours, and little time to take care of a family that needed me. So, four years ago, I made a big decision to become a professional homecare worker.

I'm deeply proud of my work, but taking this job also meant taking a big wage cut. Caretakers play an important role and we put our heart into our work, but even after forty hours a week, it's a struggle to make ends meet. I try not to think about it, but it's tough to fall asleep without worrying what will happen if _I 'm_ the one who gets sick next. Who will care for my family if I can't?

I know I'm not the only one who feels boxed in. I know so many friends and neighbors and coworkers share the same worries -- and maybe, sometimes, you do too. My mother's promise that you should be able to make ends meet if you work hard seems fainter and fainter with every paycheck.

The lawmakers we send to represent us should take this struggle seriously -- but they don't. Pat Toomey certainly isn't fighting to raise working families' wages. He isn't fighting to protect our social security, or ensure seniors can get the homecare they need, or make my grandson's school better.

It's time for a change, and I believe Katie McGinty will make that happen. She knows that we're in this together. She's working to make sure hardworking people can earn their keep. I know that she's fighting to fix the broken homecare systems that still leave too many people scared, alone, and without care.

So please, friends, join me. We can send Katie to Washington to fight for working families, but it'll take each and every one of us pulling together. **Chip in today, whatever you can, to help us put working families first again:**

**[Please, chip in to help Katie win>>](https://act.myngp.com/el/-4273185837509771264/-7280326083365306368?refcode=e20160825&refcode2=e20160825nd&amount=25)**
Thank you,

Patricia Scott,
Homecare Worker

| *** A note on donations ***

Katie is fighting for working families in PA -- but we're up against major special interest spending. We're relying on grassroots donations from people like you to win this race and take back the Senate.
**Chip in $5 today. Thank you.**

**[Donate Now](https://act.myngp.com/el/-4273185837509771264/-7280326083365306368?refcode=e20160825&refcode2=e20160825nd&amount=25)**
---

name: Cecile Richards, RussFeingold.com
subject: Even better than the cheese curds
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
I'm taking this opportunity to stand with a champion for women's health and reproductive justice: Russ Feingold. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4471/1c469400/72f27be/35742d4/84720386/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-womensweek?utm_source=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&refcode=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&utm_campaign=cr&utm_medium=ema") Friend, | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4471/1c469400/72f27be/35742d4/84720386/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-womensweek?utm_source=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&refcode=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&utm_campaign=cr&utm_medium=ema")
---
One of the best memories I have of my time in Wisconsin -- even better than the cheese curds -- is about the conversations I had with students in 2011.
Hundreds of thousands of Wisconsinites were fighting back against Governor Scott Walker's attack on our brothers and sisters in the labor movement. These students were enthusiastically organizing, because they knew the fight was bigger than one election, one candidate, or one issue. As the president of Planned Parenthood Action Fund, I was there to do whatever I could to support them.
Today, I'm standing with Wisconsin again. **I'm asking you to stand with a champion for women's health and reproductive justice: Russ Feingold. Will you join me?**
**[Russ is in a tough fight to replace one of the most anti-choice lawmakers in Congress, Senator Ron Johnson -- and he needs our help. Contribute $5 right now to support Russ.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4471/1c469400/72f27be/35742d4/84720386/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-womensweek?utm_source=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&refcode=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&utm_campaign=cr&utm_medium=ema")**
Senator Johnson has a 0 percent rating from the Planned Parenthood Action Fund. He has referred to his vote to defund Planned Parenthood as "fun." He supported legislation that could have outlawed many common forms of birth control -- including the pill -- and he co-sponsored legislation banning abortion for women facing complicated circumstances later in pregnancy.
Senator Johnson is a real and direct threat to the millions of Americans who come to Planned Parenthood health centers for compassionate care. When lawmakers like him propose cuts, or vote to defund us, they aren't just attacking Planned Parenthood -- they're attacking the 2.5 million people who come to Planned Parenthood for health care annually, some of whom are facing life-or-death situations. It's unacceptable.
**[Make no mistake: Women's health and rights are on the line and on the ballot. We have the opportunity to unseat a dangerous senator, and send a progressive champion to fight for us in the Senate -- but we need your help to get him there. Donate $5 right now to support Russ Feingold.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4471/1c469400/72f27be/35742d4/84720386/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-womensweek?utm_source=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&refcode=ema_2016-08-25_cr_nd&utm_campaign=cr&utm_medium=ema")**
Americans don't like it when politicians put their own personal politics ahead and above the lives of women. I just know that the students I met in Wisconsin in 2011 are working hard to elect progressives like Russ. I hope you are, too.
Sincerely,
Cecile Richards
Planned Parenthood Federal PAC

Thanks to _Citizens United_ , the Koch Brothers and the Fossil Fuel industry are trying to stack the deck in favor of climate deniers this November. **[We can't let them win! Our future literally depends on it. Add your name to tell Congressional Republicans to put our children's future and the health of our planet FIRST >>> ](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a3b/70cdc2ba/470a6cbb/3977ab2/1832636238/VEsH/ "http://www.climatechamps.us/petition/clean-up-politics/van-hollen/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+We+canrsquot+let+them+win+Our+future+lit&utm_campaign=em082516_1&source=em082516_1")**

\---Original Message -- Friend,

Republican members of Congress are selling out to some of the world's worst pollution offenders -- and our environment will pay the price.

Big Oil, the Koch brothers, and the Fossil Fuel industry are using their deep pockets to deter Congress from regulating pollution, making critical decisions on climate change, and supporting green energy.

Thanks to _Citizens United_ their dark money is polluting the halls of Congress more than ever before.

[**Add your name here to tell Congressional Republicans to stop taking dark money from special interests determined to oppose environmental protections.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a3b/70cdc2ba/470a6cbb/3977ab3/1832636238/VEsE/ "https://www.climatechamps.us/petition/clean-up-politics/van-hollen/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+here+to+tell+Congressional&utm_campaign=em082516_1&source=em082516_1")

We must get big polluter money out of politics. These special interests are ready to provide unlimited cash to Congressional Republicans in return for climate denial and obstructionism on our key environmental priorities.

[**For the sake of our children 's future and the health of our planet, we need to get big polluter money out of our politics. Add your name here if you agree.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91a3b/70cdc2ba/470a6cbb/3977ab0/1832636238/VEsF/ "https://www.climatechamps.us/petition/clean-up-politics/van-hollen/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+For+the+sake+of+our+childrenrsquos+futur&utm_campaign=em082516_1&source=em082516_1")

Thank you for standing up for our environment and speaking out against the spread of dark money in our political process.

Team Van Hollen
>

body:
act now for your chance to be a special guest at an exclusive campaign dinner with Donald Trump! |

Peter - I just cleared some time on my schedule for some great Americans like you to join me for dinner. You know, I’ll bet Crooked Hillary only has dinner with the lobbyists and special interests who stuff her campaign account with cash. The campaign I am running is a campaign that speaks for you. I am with you, and I am going to win this election by standing by you, not the Washington power-brokers or PACs. And I don’t care what the lying people in the media say – I am proud of you and all my supporters. I am proud of you and I respect the heck out of you, Peter. Frankly, this country would be a lot better off if people like you had more power over what goes on. So if you make a donation today, you’ll be automatically entered for you and a guest to fly to an upcoming Trump campaign fundraising dinner. Your flight and your dinner will be taken care of by me. Just come with your ideas about how I should campaign against Crooked Hillary and about what we can do to Make America Great Again. You’re a loyal supporter so I know you’d have a lot of smart things to say. I’ve told you over and over again that I will work as hard as I know how to get this job done for you. It’s true. I will never let you down. I am truly grateful for your support and I know that together, you and I can continue building our movement and win big on November 8th. All you need to do is chip in $5 or more to be automatically entered for a chance to meet me at an exciting fundraising dinner. You’re a really active friend so I wanted to make sure you got the first crack at this. Enter now! Hope to see you soon, Donald J. Trump ENTER NOW

Friend --

The coming weeks of our campaign will be the most important of this entire election. Our team has only two-and-a-half months left to reach out to voters across Indiana and get them excited about Evan's plans to bring common-sense Hoosier values to Washington.

| Peter, last night, Donald Trump went onstage at a rally and called Hillary a bigot.

I don’t know about you, but I’ve had enough of his name-calling and lies. And I’ve had enough of the days we waste in this election listening to the people on TV go back and forth about "what he really meant."

I’m so damn tired of us tiptoeing around this -- the election is in 75 days, and a person who gets in bed with the most dangerous fringe right-wing white supremacists in America could really and truly be our president! The time to be polite is behind us. This is a time to take decisive, meaningful action.

**[Which is why you need to chip in today. (And, yes, you will get your free sticker as a thank you.)](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjYzNDA1OTYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0NTA2NzMwS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

I want to tell Hillary how you came through and show her that we all have her back.

**You know our fundraising has been lagging behind in August -- and we only have a few days left to catch up. So let’s get going right now and make an indelible statement about who we are and what we stand for:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjYzNDA1OTYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODY0NTA2NzMwS0&mt=1&rt=0)

subject: Eric Greitens
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Democrats Attack Greitens Days Before Missouri GOP Primary

By Maggie Severns, POLITICO
7/28/2016 5:17 PM EST

A political group affiliated with the Democratic Governors Association is reserving ad time to attack GOP gubernatorial candidate Eric Greitens in Missouri, seeking to affect the race just five days before Republicans choose their nominee from a crowded field.

Two sources tracking media spending in the state said that Jobs and Opportunity, a 527 group funded by the DGA, has booked about a half-million dollars in broadcast TV and additional radio spending attacking Greitens. The DGA did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The ad is set to start airing Friday. Audio, which a source said was from the ad, criticizes Greitens for accepting money from a donor accused of sexually abusing a woman.

"What does this victim accused of human trafficking have to do with your vote for governor? Well a man accused of abusing her for 13 years gave Eric Greitens $1 million," a narrator says in the ad. "While others returned his money, Greitens said he was honored to take it."

Greitens, a former Navy SEAL and political newcomer, has been picking up steam in the competitive GOP primary because of his charisma and status as an outsider. The ad is a clear indication that Democrats would rather not face Greitens in the general election. Polls have found the race between Greitens and the three other candidates extremely close, but a SurveyUSA poll released this week did find Greitens leading the pack with 25 percent, with opponent John Brunner behind him at 21 percent.

Missouri was the site of a notorious instance of Democratic primary meddling four years ago, when Democratic Sen. Claire McCaskill's campaign aired ads casting GOP Rep. Todd Akin as conservative before his Senate primary. Akin won the three-way Republican race and promptly imploded in the general election, losing to McCaskill by more than 15 percentage points.

The DGA tried to influence Republicans' gubernatorial primary in Colorado in 2014, funding a group that tried to boost conservative ex-Rep. Tom Tancredo. But the effort prompted a backlash and Tancredo ultimately lost the GOP primary.

The winner in the Missouri GOP primary will face current Attorney General Chris Koster, a moderate and rising star in the Democratic Party, in the general election.

Chip in now to help us fight back (https://donate.ericgreitens.com)

St. Louis, Missouri 63108

clean_body: Democrats Attack Greitens Days Before Missouri GOP Primary By Maggie Severns, POLITICO 7/28/2016 5:17 PM EST A political group affiliated with the Democratic Governors Association is reserving ad time to attack GOP gubernatorial candidate Eric Greitens in Missouri, seeking to affect the race just five days before Republicans choose their nominee from a crowded field. Two sources tracking media spending in the state said that Jobs and Opportunity, a 527 group funded by the DGA, has booked about a half-million dollars in broadcast TV and additional radio spending attacking Greitens. The DGA did not immediately respond to a request for comment. The ad is set to start airing Friday. Audio, which a source said was from the ad, criticizes Greitens for accepting money from a donor accused of sexually abusing a woman. "What does this victim accused of human trafficking have to do with your vote for governor? Well a man accused of abusing her for 13 years gave Eric Greitens $1 million," a narrator says in the ad. "While others returned his money, Greitens said he was honored to take it." Greitens, a former Navy SEAL and political newcomer, has been picking up steam in the competitive GOP primary because of his charisma and status as an outsider. The ad is a clear indication that Democrats would rather not face Greitens in the general election. Polls have found the race between Greitens and the three other candidates extremely close, but a SurveyUSA poll released this week did find Greitens leading the pack with 25 percent, with opponent John Brunner behind him at 21 percent. Missouri was the site of a notorious instance of Democratic primary meddling four years ago, when Democratic Sen. Claire McCaskill's campaign aired ads casting GOP Rep. Todd Akin as conservative before his Senate primary. Akin won the three-way Republican race and promptly imploded in the general election, losing to McCaskill by more than 15 percentage points. The DGA tried to influence Republicans' gubernatorial primary in Colorado in 2014, funding a group that tried to boost conservative ex-Rep. Tom Tancredo. But the effort prompted a backlash and Tancredo ultimately lost the GOP primary. The winner in the Missouri GOP primary will face current Attorney General Chris Koster, a moderate and rising star in the Democratic Party, in the general election.
